summary: Executed backend + frontend regression on public URL for content revamp. Backend APIs passed; one frontend-backend blog metadata integration bug found.

integration_issues.issue: Read time is missing in UI because frontend reads post.readTime while backend provides read_time.

integration_issues.flow: Product detail file slot rendering from /api/site-content
integration_issues.issue: Frontend reads product.fileSlots while backend payload key is file_slots; backend-customized slot names may not render.

action_items: In Blog page, map backend read_time to UI (or normalize API response) so read time appears.
In ProductDetail page, map backend file_slots to frontend fileSlots (or normalize once in hook/API layer).
Retest Blog and ProductDetail on public URL after key normalization.
critical_code_review_comments: Schema drift exists between backend snake_case fields and frontend camelCase usage for blog and product-detail display fields.
useSiteContent is called separately in Layout and each page, causing repeated /api/site-content fetches; central provider/state would reduce redundant network calls.
seed_data_creation: Created test contact/newsletter records through API during regression checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All required revamp sections/routes and site-content CRUD passed. Repro pending bug 1: Blog meta shows date only (no read time) on /blog. Repro pending bug 2: ProductDetail uses fileSlots key while API uses file_slots; confirm with products having custom file slots.
rca of the issue: RCA-1: Backend returns blog entries with key read_time; frontend Blog.jsx renders post.readTime, so metadata prints as 'date •' without duration. RCA-2: Backend product payload uses file_slots, but ProductDetail.jsx reads product.fileSlots; this prevents backend-customized file slot labels from appearing and may fall back to generic values.
